behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Depends on real LLM content quality and grounding behavior. PROOF-BUNDLE: forcing phrase is "references specific points ... rather than producing a generic response." At Integration level with stubbed model outputs, the test can pass while the real model still returns vague/generic replies; this is a model-judgment failure, not generic wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real LLM on representative real email threads; assert the draft cites thread-specific entities/details (e.g., named requests, dates, prior commitments) and fails a specificity rubric when output is generic.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff: claim extraction/checker + KB lookup + contradiction flagging. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no required real AI/ML judgment in the behavior text, no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is deterministic contradiction detection against KB entries, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real fact-checking service with a test KB and drafted text containing known contradictions; verify contradiction flags are emitted with correct claim-to-KB mapping.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM behavior under missing context (hallucination vs disclaimer). PROOF-BUNDLE: forcing phrase is "includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts." At Integration with deterministic stubs, disclaimer behavior is forced and cannot reveal real hallucination/fabrication risk; this is specifically model-decision dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute generation with real LLM and empty/irrelevant KB retrieval results; assert presence of required uncertainty disclaimer and absence of unsupported factual assertions across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at orchestration boundary between draft state, approval state, and send action. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: does not require real OS/network/subprocess to catch gating logic; does not depend on AI/ML output; no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about approval gating, which is verifiable by asserting sender module is not invoked before explicit approval.
behaviors.test_description: Run workflow modules together with a fake sender adapter; verify send is never called before an explicit approval event and is called exactly once after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full pipeline over multiple stages, including an agentic generation stage and downstream handoff correctness. MULTI-STEP applies: full agent pipeline sequence is the failure boundary, not any single component.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end on a real email thread through real pipeline components (thread ingestion, LLM drafting, fact-check stage, approval presentation) and verify stage outputs and transitions complete correctly without bypassing approval.
